duration: intermittently with photos taken early afternoon
specific_location: 5 miles South from the South edge of Lamar on Hwy 287 and 1/4 mile to the west in the back yard of farm home. Open plains (crop land and pasture) surround farm headquarters.
description: -Overall greenish bird with whitish underparts -Overall length about 3.5 -Appeared smaller and rounder with a thick neck -Relatively short, curved bill -Green Crown, whitish supercillium -Short tail -One photo shows wing length about the same as tail length (but may be slightly tucked under tail) -Another photo appears to show wing length longer than tailBehaviors: The bird fed longer at one time at feeder than other hummingbirds I've observed; it also buzzed over the blooming plants, but drank from the feeder.Call: Not notedPlumage: Female or Immature maybe molting
photographer: Photographer: Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp
